title: 11 Projects Receive Subsidy for Production of Sustainable Hydrogen
content: The government is providing more than â‚¬700 million in subsidies to companies that will produce sustainable hydrogen in the Netherlands. The government will use this to accelerate the development of sustainable hydrogen in the Netherlands. This increases security of supply, contributes to making industry and mobility more sustainable, and reduces CO2-emission. In addition to increasing the supply, efforts are also being made to stimulate use by industry, refineries and in mobility. The money comes from the OWE subsidy scheme that opened last year. A total of â‚¬3.2 billion in subsidies were requested, of which more than â‚¬700 million was awarded to 11 companies. The subsidized projects account for about 602 megawatts of electrolysis capacity. In terms of production volume, this is more than three times as much as the currently largest hydrogen plant under construction, the Holland Hydrogen 1.
author: Analysts Account
